summary: Recommends artifact-only integration: BimodalLogic exports JSONL via compiled Lake executable (infrastructure already exists in DataExport.lean + DatasetGenerator.lean + FormulaEnumerator.lean); BimodalHarness reads static files. ModelChecker consumed as pip package model-checker==1.2.12. No git submodule. Lean v4.27.0-rc1 is a BimodalLogic-only concern; BimodalHarness has no Lean runtime dependency for static pipeline.

artifacts.summary: Full architecture decision record: artifact-only coupling, JSONL schema, ModelChecker pip dependency, version compatibility matrix, risk register, boundary definitions, integration point table by task

memory_candidates.content: BimodalLogic (Lean v4.27.0-rc1) has a complete data export stack already implemented: DataExport.lean provides JSON serializers for Formula (6 primitive constructors: atom/bot/imp/box/untl/snce), PatternKey, SimpleCountermodel, and RuleProfile. DatasetGenerator.lean provides LabeledFormula struct with label (valid/invalid/timeout), proofTrace, countermodel, DifficultyMetrics, and PatternKey. FormulaEnumerator.lean provides configurable exhaustive/random/hybrid formula generation. The BimodalLogic task 203 plan targets a compiled 'lake exe dataset_generator' that streams JSONL.

ModelChecker bimodal theory is published as pip package 'model-checker==1.2.12' (Python >= 3.8, z3-solver >= 4.8.0). The bimodal module at model_checker.theory_lib.bimodal exposes BimodalSemantics (N world-states, M time-points, Z3 BitVec encoding), BimodalStructure, BimodalProposition. This is a richer countermodel source than the Lean SimpleCountermodel (which is atom-level only). For Task 19 (Z3 countermodel generator), use model-checker as a pip dependency rather than reimplementing semantics from scratch.

Artifact-only integration pattern for Lean-to-Python coupling: Lean exports JSONL/Parquet via compiled lake executable; Python reads static files. No git submodule needed. Data sync via rsync or Makefile target during development; GitHub Releases or git LFS for CI reproducibility. This decouples Lean toolchain from Python CI and allows version-pinned dataset snapshots. The pattern is preferable to git submodule when the interface is purely file-based.

next_steps: Run /plan 3 to create implementation plan, or run /research 5 to coordinate BimodalLogic export format (depends on this task)
